attributes.title: Elastic ML Anomaly Monitor
attributes.description: Starter dashboard for order-flow and signal-drift anomalies.
attributes.visState:
{"title":"Elastic ML Anomaly Monitor","type":"markdown","aggs":[],"params":{"markdown":"# Elastic ML Anomaly Monitor

## 1. Anomaly Timeline
Lens suggestion: line chart from `.ml-anomalies-*` filtered to `result_type: record` and `record_score >= 50`, split series by `job_id`, x-axis on `timestamp`.

## 2. Per-Market Heatmap
Lens suggestion: heatmap from `.ml-anomalies-*` filtered to `partition_field_name: market_id`, rows=`partition_field_value`, columns=`date histogram(timestamp, 5m)`, metric=`max(record_score)`.

## 3. Signal Degradation Tracker
Lens suggestion: table filtered to `job_id: elastifund-signal-confidence-drift`, break down by `partition_field_value`, show `max(record_score)` and latest `actual` vs `typical`.

## 4. Correlation View
Build an overlay in Lens or TSVB using `elastifund-trades` cumulative PnL against `.ml-anomalies-*` max `record_score` to show whether stress periods align with drawdowns.

## Runtime Notes
- Spread anomaly should map to temporary market pauses in `bot/anomaly_consumer.py`.
- VPIN and OFI anomalies should map to position-size reductions in `bot/jj_live.py`.
- Kill-rule frequency anomaly is an operator alert, not a directional trade signal.","fontSize":12,"openLinksInNewTab":true}}
